title: Intel macからsilicon MBPに変更したため、intel mac用のアプリのエラーが多発している。徹底的に調べてエラーが出ないようにして
summary_text: 1. `Docker Desktop` is the only currently active auto-start item I found with a verified Intel helper. `launchctl print gui/$(id -u)` shows `application.com.docker.docker...`, `com.docker.helper`, and `com.docker.osxfs`, and [`/Applications/Docker.app/Contents/MacOS/com.docker.osxfs`](/Applications/Docker.app/Contents/MacOS/com.docker.osxfs) is `x86_64` while the main backend/virtualization binaries are arm64. This is the first thing I would fix because it is running now and comes back on login. 2. [`Cisco Webex Meetings.app`](/Applications/Cisco%20Webex%20Meetings.app) is `x86_64` only. It is not in login items, but if you use meetings often, it is one of the highest daily-impact Intel apps left. 3. [`Screen Studio.app`](/Applications/Screen%20Studio.app) and [`tldv.app`](/Applications/tldv.app) are both `x86_64` only. They are not auto-starting from what I saw, but they are high-frequency work apps if you record or capture meetings. 4. The ScanSnap stack is still Intel-heavy: [`ABBYY FineReader for ScanSnap.app`](/Applications/ABBYY%20FineReader%20for%20ScanSnap/ABBYY%20FineReader%20for%20ScanSnap.app), [`ScanSnapHomeMain.app`](/Applications/ScanSnapHomeMain.app), and the helpers under [`ScanSnap Online Update.localized`](/Applications/ScanSnap%20Online%20Update.localized) are `x86_64` / `i386`. If you use the scanner regularly, this is a major remaining source of Rosetta activity. 5. [`R.app`](/Applications/R.app) and [`Stata/StataMP.app`](/Applications/Stata/StataMP.app) are `x86_64` only. These are high priority only if you use them day to day. 6. [`Paperpile.app`](/Applications/Paperpile.app) and [`Papers.app`](/Applications/Papers.app) are `x86_64` only. Same story: medium priority unless they are part of your daily workflow. 7. Lower-priority Intel-only apps still present are [`Chronist.app`](/Applications/Chronist.app), [`COEIROINKv2.app`](/Applications/COEIROINKv2.app), [`KAMUI-4D.app`](/Applications/KAMUI-4D.app), [`PassFab For Excel.app`](/Applications/PassFab%20For%20Excel.app), and [`AnswerSheetDIY.app`](/Applications/AnswerSheetDIY.app). They matter if you actually launch them often, otherwise they are not the first source of instability. No verified login item surfaced as x86-only. The ones I checked are already okay: `Google Drive` is arm64, `Aqua Voice` is arm64, and `Paste` / `CleanShot X` / `Dropover` are universal.
